targets.current_owner: TA Associates; Partners Group and management minority

evidence.note: Reuters reported that TA hired JPMorgan to run a sale, with binding bids expected in June and an asking value of up to USD 2bn. Bain Capital and General Atlantic were identified as interested parties.
TA's official announcement describes a profitable franchise-led global tea platform and confirms TA's investment alongside Partners Group, management and regional founders.

targets.next_action: Confirm whether the June binding-bid timetable remains live, map the current bidder set, and test a differentiated HSG Asia-to-global operating angle before the process closes.
watch_triggers: China and Southeast Asia unit economics

evidence.note: Jacobs Capital was reported to be exploring a sale for more than EUR 500m with Houlihan Lokey advising. Deliberations were described as preliminary.
Telemos acquired Mammut for its premium brand, technical credentials, digital channels and international growth potential; Telemos and Jacobs Holding later combined as Jacobs Capital.

targets.next_action: Assess the reported sale now: confirm process status and buyer list, test whether the sub-scale valuation can fit HSG, and underwrite APAC distribution and DTC upside.
targets.sub_sector: premium technical outdoor equipment and apparel

evidence.note: Investindustrial identifies Guala as a current portfolio company acquired in August 2021. The business is a global leader in specialty closures, with products sold in more than 100 countries.
Company investor materials show more than EUR 900m of revenue and publicly traded senior secured notes due in 2028 and 2029.

targets.next_action: Build the pre-process angle now: map Investindustrial and management, review the 2028/2029 debt stack, and test HSG value creation through Asia growth and procurement.

related_signals.excerpt: Guala Closures S.p.A. is a single-shareholder company held by Special Packaging Solutions Investments S.à r.l. (an independently managed investment subsidiary of Investindustrial VII L.P.) which, within its role of shareholder, supports the Group's business activities, fostering their shared views and commitments.

evidence.note: PAI's current portfolio lists Armacell with a February 2020 investment date. PAI and KIRKBI acquired the company from Blackstone for its global leadership in flexible equipment insulation and engineered foams.

targets.next_action: Map PAI and KIRKBI decision makers, establish current EV and EBITDA, and underwrite whether energy-efficiency regulation plus Asia capacity can support a sponsor-to-sponsor exit.
targets.sub_sector: advanced insulation and engineered foam products

targets.current_owner: CVC Capital Partners Asia V and I.T founder group (co-control)

evidence.note: CVC's current portfolio continues to list A Bathing Ape. CVC acquired co-control in 2021 after the I.T privatization, with online and geographic expansion in China, the US and Europe as stated value-creation priorities.

targets.next_action: Open a sponsor map with CVC Asia and the I.T founder group, establish current EBITDA and ownership economics, and test whether brand heat converts into durable full-price sell-through.

evidence.note: Bloomberg reported that IK was working with Bank of America to gauge interest in a potential sale valuing Kersia at up to EUR 2bn; the process was described as early-stage.
IK's official portfolio still identifies Kersia as current, acquired in December 2020, with 23 manufacturing sites and operations in 130 countries.

targets.next_action: Re-test whether the 2025 sale preparation is active, then build a narrower HSG angle around Asia food safety, regulated formulations and buy-and-build rather than accepting the reported headline value.

evidence.note: PAI's current portfolio lists StellaGroup as a January 2019 investment and a European leader in sun-protection and closure systems.
Mergermarket reported that PAI was preparing a 2H25 exit. No completion has been verified, so the system treats this as a stale-process reactivation candidate rather than a live auction.

targets.next_action: Call around the uncompleted 2025 process, establish current leverage and construction exposure, and identify whether a bilateral sponsor solution is more plausible than a relaunched auction.

evidence.note: Schleich's 2026 company update says it remains owned by Partners Group and employees, while highlighting a 2025 transformation, supply-chain redesign and upcoming finance and sales leadership appointments.

Bloomberg reported creditor and debt-restructuring talks that could have transferred control. Current company disclosure indicates Partners Group still owns the business, but the operating and leverage reset remains a core diligence issue.

targets.next_action: Treat the brand as attractive but the asset as unproven: verify the post-2025 capital structure, revenue stabilization and supply-chain reset before considering any ownership conversation.

related_signals.excerpt: Impressive creatures from the Jurassic, Cretaceous, and Ice Age: schleich® brings dinosaurs and other prehistoric creatures back from the past. With these detailed figurines, dinosaur fans can travel back several million years in time and experience exciting adventures.

targets.sub_sector: premium toy figurines and play worlds

evidence.note: PAI continues to list Albéa as a current investment acquired in March 2018. Market reporting says PAI has weighed a sale after acquiring the business for approximately USD 1.6bn.

S&P rates Albéa B-/Negative and describes high leverage and weak competitive positioning. The packaging adjacency is attractive, but credit deterioration makes this a diligence-gated situation rather than a clean Guala lookalike.

targets.next_action: Start with the capital structure and segment-level margins, not the sale story. Determine whether tubes and cosmetics components can be separated and whether HSG has a credible operational reset case.

targets.current_owner: HPS Investment Partners and Arcmont Asset Management

evidence.note: HPS and Arcmont acquired 100% after creditor negotiations, injected EUR 30m of equity and reduced debt by EUR 190m. Carlyle exited following the restructuring.
Vienna MTF confirms that ISIN XS2481126204 was extended and resized to EUR 142.1m of senior secured floating-rate notes due 2030.

targets.next_action: Track the post-restructuring operating recovery and the 2030 notes. Do not pursue as a normal sponsor exit until revenue, margin and liquidity stabilize under the new creditor owners.

Dainese Announces Significant Investment from HPS Investment Partners and Arcmont Asset Management

Dainese S.p.A. (“Dainese” or the “Company”), a global leader in motorcycle protective gear and apparel, today announced that the Company has secured a significant investment from its existing investors: HPS Investment Partners (“HPS”) and Arcmont Asset Management (“Arcmont”). As part of the transaction, HPS and Arcmont will become majority shareholders in the business.

“We are excited to begin this next chapter of our growth alongside HPS and Arcmont, both of which have been strong and loyal supporters of our Company. Following their significant investment in Dainese earlier in the year, their continued support will enable Dainese to focus on sustainable performance and long-term value creation as we continue to serve our loyal customers. targets.current_owner: Astorg and Montagu (majority); LGT Capital Partners minority

evidence.note: Astorg and Montagu sold a minority stake to LGT Capital Partners but retained joint majority ownership, creating a fresh valuation and liquidity marker after six years of ownership.
Astorg describes Nemera as a global leader in drug-delivery devices, with proprietary and contract-manufacturing models and EUR 369m of sales at acquisition.

targets.next_action: Use the 2025 LGT minority transaction to infer valuation and governance, then determine whether the majority owners are extending the hold or preparing a broader exit.

evidence.note: Jacobs Capital lists Vittoria as a current majority investment entered in December 2022 and describes it as a leading premium performance bicycle-tyre brand with proprietary compounds and advanced manufacturing.

targets.next_action: Build the thesis before the exit window: quantify replacement-driven recurring demand, premium mix, OEM versus aftermarket economics, and Asia manufacturing and distribution exposure.
targets.sub_sector: premium performance bicycle tyres and accessories

ai_evidence.note: The reported valuation process identifies data-center and grid electrical engineering as the core growth exposure; direct customer concentration and AI-related backlog still require diligence.

targets.ai_thesis: Hyperscale and colocation data-center build-outs require high-voltage design, grid connections and mission-critical electrical delivery; H&MV sells the physical capacity layer rather than speculative AI software.

evidence.note: Reported as approaching roughly EUR 1bn valuation as Exponent considers a single-asset continuation/stake restructuring. Paywalled public source; verify before outreach.
PE Hub Europe headline reported Warburg Pincus to exit Singular Bank. Verify valuation, process status, and buyer universe.

ai_evidence.note: Official materials support exposure to logistics, automation and a recurring installed base; the incremental revenue attributable to AI-led automation remains an explicit diligence gap.

idea_queue.ai_thesis: Warehouse automation, robotics and higher-throughput logistics increase belt density and replacement demand across AMMEGA's installed base; this is an indirect industrial-automation beneficiary, not an AI premium by itself.
idea_queue.business_model: Recurring small-ticket conveyor and power-transmission belts sold into a large installed base across food, pharma and logistics.

evidence.note: Partners Group identifies AMMEGA as a current 2018 investment with recurring sales of mission-critical, small-ticket consumables to approximately 50,000 customers in more than 50 countries.

idea_queue.next_action: Establish current EBITDA, leverage and EV, map Partners Group decision makers, and identify any refinancing, continuation or adviser activity.
idea_queue.why_now: Partners Group still lists AMMEGA as current after an eight-year hold; the business has unusually strong PE-control characteristics and a credible Asia operating angle.

idea_queue.business_model: Premium property brokerage brand and software-services platform used by independent agents in more than 35 countries.

evidence.note: Permira lists Engel & Völkers as a current 2021 investment and describes a global premium real-estate software, services and brokerage-brand platform.

idea_queue.next_action: Verify current valuation and recurring platform economics, split transaction-linked commissions from software and service revenue, and map Permira and family governance.
idea_queue.why_now: Permira confirms current ownership and a near-five-year hold; the brand, independent-agent model and digital platform create a differentiated asset-light control thesis.

idea_queue.business_model: Integrated airfield, gate and tower systems that improve airport capacity and safety, supported by a mission-critical installed base.

evidence.note: Carlyle's official portfolio records an October 2017 investment and current status for the integrated airfield-guidance leader.

idea_queue.next_action: Confirm revenue and service mix, current leverage and EV, and whether Carlyle is considering a sale, continuation vehicle or refinancing.
idea_queue.why_now: Carlyle still marks the asset current after almost nine years, making ownership duration itself a high-priority exit signal.

ai_evidence.note: Partners Group explicitly identifies machine learning, AI and 5G as demand drivers and describes atNorth as a renewable-only Nordic data-center and HPC platform.

idea_queue.ai_thesis: AI training and inference require high-density power and cooling; atNorth sells renewable compute capacity to HPC and enterprise customers rather than taking model or application risk.
idea_queue.business_model: Pan-Nordic colocation and high-performance-computing data centers using low-cost renewable power, efficient cooling and long-lived customer infrastructure.

evidence.note: Investec confirms Partners Group ownership and additional senior debt following an initial 2024 financing, describing atNorth as a rapidly growing Nordic operator with a gigawatt-scale power bank.

idea_queue.next_action: Establish current sold MW, contracted backlog, customer concentration, senior debt and EV, then map Partners Group infrastructure decision makers and any 2026-27 liquidity plan.
idea_queue.thesis_cluster: AI infrastructure / renewable HPC data centers
idea_queue.why_now: Partners Group acquired atNorth in December 2021, putting the asset near a conventional five-year review window. Investec added senior growth debt in 2024 and 2025, but current EV and sold capacity remain open.

idea_queue.business_model: Distribution and care solutions for chronic-care patients and healthcare professionals, with recurring medical-device consumption and home delivery.

evidence.note: Mediq's official corporate-governance page states that Advent has owned the business since 2013 and repositioned it around outpatient medical devices and care solutions.

idea_queue.next_action: Map Advent's current ownership vehicle, size the retained medical-device business, and test whether a divisional or sponsor-to-sponsor transaction is feasible.
idea_queue.why_now: The company still identifies Advent as its investor after a thirteen-year hold; this is an unusually mature sponsor asset but size, leverage and carve-out complexity are unresolved.

idea_queue.business_model: Profitable specialty-pharma platform focused on pain, addiction, critical care and hospital injectables, with direct sales across major European markets.

evidence.note: PAI lists Ethypharm as a current July 2016 investment; company materials describe profitable growth and a direct commercial presence across Europe's five largest markets.

idea_queue.next_action: Establish current product-level EBITDA, patent and generic exposure, EV and leverage, then map PAI's intended exit route.
idea_queue.why_now: PAI's official portfolio still lists the 2016 investment; the ten-year hold and expansion into direct commercialization make it a credible exit-window candidate.

ai_evidence.note: The company confirms it is a dedicated European turnkey data-center delivery provider; the AI linkage comes through hyperscale physical capacity and must be measured in backlog rather than marketing language.

idea_queue.ai_thesis: Winthrop monetizes the physical build-out of hyperscale and AI capacity through specialist execution, but backlog concentration, fixed-price risk and customer bargaining power require disciplined underwriting.
idea_queue.business_model: Dedicated turnkey data-center delivery partner providing design, construction and commissioning for hyperscale customers across Europe.

evidence.note: Official company and EU competition materials confirm Blackstone control at 50.7% with founders and management retaining 49.3%. A William Fry transaction review reports EUR 406m for the controlling stake.

evidence_gaps: current enterprise value and net debt
idea_queue.next_action: Build a 2024 entry bridge from equity value to EV, quantify hyperscaler backlog and customer concentration, and establish a Blackstone relationship ahead of a 2028-29 exit window.
idea_queue.next_gate: Wait for mature hold or exit signal
idea_queue.why_now: Blackstone acquired 50.7% in 2024 at a reported EUR 406m stake value, implying approximately EUR 800m equity value. The size is highly relevant, but the ownership period is still too young for an exit assumption.

idea_queue.business_model: Garment labels, tags, packaging, RFID and supply-chain traceability solutions for global apparel and footwear brands.

evidence.note: Affinity reported its sale of Trimco to Brookfield at an enterprise value of USD 850m; Trimco's own materials confirm Brookfield ownership and a global brand-identity and traceability platform.

idea_queue.next_action: Track hold duration, RFID and ProductDNA adoption, customer concentration and any Brookfield refinancing while building an Asia supply-chain thesis.
idea_queue.next_gate: Wait for mature hold or exit signal
idea_queue.why_now: The USD 850m Brookfield acquisition sits directly in the target size band and the business resembles Guala, but the four-year hold is not yet enough to assume an exit.

ai_evidence.note: I Squared describes nLighten as its European edge data-center platform and directly links the build-out to AI-driven compute demand.

idea_queue.ai_thesis: AI inference shifts part of compute demand toward lower-latency regional capacity; nLighten owns the physical data-center layer and has diversified city-level customers rather than a generic software claim.
idea_queue.business_model: Pan-European edge data-center platform supplying low-latency capacity to enterprise and hyperscale customers in regional markets.

evidence.note: I Squared disclosed more than USD 500m of committed equity for nLighten's expansion and positioned the platform as regional European edge infrastructure.
evidence.source: I Squared Capital / IPE Real Assets reprint

idea_queue.next_action: Establish current invested capital, contracted MW, utilization, customer concentration and EV; track follow-on acquisitions and any capital-partner or refinancing event.
idea_queue.why_now: I Squared still identifies nLighten as a current platform and disclosed more than USD 500m of committed expansion equity; the platform could approach HSG's target band as it scales, but a 2023 formation makes an imminent exit unlikely.

idea_queue.business_model: Japanese pure-play pharmaceutical contract manufacturing, packaging and clinical-trial materials platform.

evidence.note: KKR's current portfolio materials state that Bushu joined KKR in February 2023 and is being expanded across capacity, quality control and organic and inorganic growth.

idea_queue.next_action: Track capacity additions, quality record, customer concentration and add-ons; begin sponsor mapping once the hold passes four years or a financing signal appears.
idea_queue.why_now: KKR confirms current ownership and operating investment, but the 2023 entry is too recent for an assumed exit and should be developed as an early thesis.

ai_evidence.note: Seraya states that the USD 828m commitment will fund more than 200MW in Seoul, Tokyo and Southeast Asia and explicitly targets AI workload demand.

idea_queue.ai_thesis: Empyrion provides high-density power, cooling and connectivity for Asian AI workloads in Seoul, Tokyo and Southeast Asia; construction, leasing and power-allocation risk remain central.
idea_queue.business_model: Pan-Asian low-carbon data-center owner and operator with operating and under-construction capacity in supply-constrained Tier 1 and emerging markets.

evidence.note: Seraya confirms 100% ownership through Fund I and reports 290MW of operating and under-construction capacity across the pan-Asia platform.

idea_queue.next_action: Map Seraya and co-investor governance, quantify operating versus construction MW and contracted tenants, and monitor for asset-level debt, secondary liquidity or a platform-level continuation process.
idea_queue.thesis_cluster: AI infrastructure / pan-Asia green data centers
idea_queue.why_now: The original 2021 platform is approaching five years, but the USD 828m 2025 co-investment effectively resets the capital clock. This is an HSG relationship-development idea rather than a near-term auction candidate.

ai_evidence.note: Ardian identifies AI, machine learning and LLM workloads as core demand and disclosed a plan to nearly quadruple Verne's 29MW of sold capacity.

idea_queue.ai_thesis: Verne combines low-carbon power, dense compute capacity and recurring infrastructure contracts for AI and HPC users; development execution and capital intensity are the key PE-control risks.
idea_queue.business_model: Renewable-powered Northern European data centers optimized for high-performance computing, AI and enterprise workloads with recurring contracted revenue.

evidence.note: Digital 9 disclosed an equity purchase price of up to USD 575m; Ardian confirms 100% ownership and up to USD 1.2bn of expansion funding through equity and debt.

idea_queue.next_action: Track sold versus live MW, expansion drawdown, customer concentration and green financing; revisit when the 2024 underwriting plan matures or Ardian brings in a capital partner.
idea_queue.thesis_cluster: AI infrastructure / low-carbon HPC data centers
idea_queue.why_now: Ardian paid up to USD 575m for equity in 2024 and committed up to USD 1.2bn of equity and debt for expansion. The platform is strategically relevant but the new ownership and large build plan push a likely exit beyond the near term.

ai_evidence.note: Official disclosures describe the assets as infrastructure for AI and high-performance-computing workloads and identify the use of bond proceeds.

idea_queue.ai_thesis: Polar owns the physical power and cooling layer for AI compute; its new secured bond creates unusually transparent credit monitoring, while development and tenant concentration risk need underwriting.
idea_queue.business_model: Developer, owner and operator of AI-ready European data centers targeting high-performance computing, hyperscale and cloud workloads.

event_signals.note: EUR 800m first-lien senior secured Nordic bond issued to refinance Drangedal and fund two additional facilities.

evidence.note: H.I.G. confirms a controlling investment in Polar in October 2024 and continues to list it as a current portfolio company.

idea_queue.next_action: Obtain the final bond prospectus, ISIN, coupon, maturity, price and covenant package; track construction completion, leasing and leverage before considering sponsor outreach.
idea_queue.why_now: H.I.G. acquired control in October 2024 and Polar issued an EUR 800m senior secured bond in June 2026. The debt signal makes the platform observable, but the young hold and construction program rule out a near-term exit thesis.

ai_evidence.note: Official portfolio materials confirm current ownership and mission-critical high-voltage products; the AI linkage is an inference from incremental grid demand and must be quantified before promotion.

idea_queue.ai_thesis: AI data-center load growth increases grid reinforcement and high-voltage interconnection requirements; Trench supplies difficult-to-substitute components, though AI is one demand driver among broader electrification themes.
idea_queue.business_model: Global manufacturer of mission-critical high-voltage bushings, instrument transformers and coils used to keep transmission grids stable and connected.

evidence.note: Triton completed the Siemens Energy carve-out in April 2024 and identifies Trench as a current portfolio company with 2,400 employees and factories across Europe, North America and China.

idea_queue.next_action: Use as an AI-power-infrastructure comp, estimate current EV and EBITDA, map data-center/grid backlog, and revisit when hold duration or refinancing indicates an exit window.
idea_queue.why_now: This is a strong category-learning asset with current Triton ownership and China manufacturing exposure, but the 2024 carve-out is too young to be an exit-window target.

rejected_memory.reason: The company states it returned to full ownership by the Lam family after KKR, so it is not a current sponsor-held acquisition target.
Apollo completed its acquisition of a majority stake in January 2026; the prior Triton exit window is closed.
Partners Group sold Straive to BPEA in 2021; it cannot be treated as a current Partners Group asset.

A March 2026 fund-to-fund acquisition financing and EUR 1.3bn refinancing reset the exit clock and indicate a platform likely above HSG's core size band. Preserve it as a transformer-category reference, not a current outreach target.
